itinerary.mapNote: Hot springs at Lares before the climb. First high camp beside a glacial lake. 4200m.

itinerary.route: Ipsaycocha to Cancha Cancha via high pass
itinerary.mapNote: Highest point of the trek at 4600m. Descent to Cancha Cancha — a Quechua weaving community. 4600m.

itinerary.route: Cancha Cancha to Patacancha via cloud forest
itinerary.mapNote: Cloud forest descent past alpaca herds and terracing. Patacancha textile market. 3760m.

itinerary.route: Patacancha to Ollantaytambo, train to Aguas Calientes, Machu Picchu
itinerary.mapNote: Final descent to Ollantaytambo Inca fortress. Train through the Sacred Valley to Machu Picchu. 2430m.

waypoints.name: Lares Hot Springs
waypoints.note: Natural thermal pools at the trek's start — traditional bathing site for local communities.

waypoints.name: Ipsaycocha Lake
waypoints.note: High glacial lake camp. Condors frequently visible above the surrounding peaks.

waypoints.name: Trek High Pass
waypoints.note: The Lares Trek's highest point at 4600m. Full Andean panorama in clear conditions.

waypoints.name: Cancha Cancha
waypoints.note: Traditional Quechua weaving community — meet weavers using techniques unchanged for centuries.

waypoints.name: Ollantaytambo
waypoints.note: Major Inca fortress and living Inca town — the Sacred Valley's finest ruins before Machu Picchu.

waypoints.name: Machu Picchu
waypoints.note: The Lares Trek's finish — arriving by train through the cloud forest is one of South America's great reveals.
